title: Fishhawk work-management conventions v0
description: Schema for a repo's work-management conventions config (#1005). This is a NEW canonical artifact, NOT a block inside .fishhawk/workflows.yaml (workflow-v0 is frozen at Day 21). It declares the work-management provider (GitHub Projects today; Jira deferred), its connection, and a map of work-item types (bug / feature / chore / adr / …) — each with a title format, body skeleton, default labels/fields, board placement, numbering rule, and epic-link policy. The conventions layer is the value: it turns one tool call into a conventions-complete work item. The shipped default (docs/spec/work-management-default.yaml) is a product artifact validated against this schema at backend package init. Breaking changes bump to work-management-v1 in a new schema file; old configs stay readable forever.

spec_version.description: Config schema version. Single-value enum per the docs/spec versioning rules; a breaking change goes to work-management-v1 in a new schema file.

provider.description: Work-management backend. github_projects is the only concrete provider in v0; jira is reserved at the interface level (the model must be expressible as a Jira issue) but has no implementation yet — an unimplemented provider must fail closed at filing time, never silently.

project.description: GitHub Projects connection. Required when provider is github_projects. The jira provider has its own connection block (`jira`).

owner_type.description: Whether the owner login is a user or an organization. GitHub's GraphQL owner query differs by kind (the Project #7 trap: kuhlman-labs is a user, not an org).

number.description: Project number (the integer in the project URL).

jira.description: Jira connection. Required when provider is jira (semantic check). This block selects ONLY the target project; the Jira instance base URL and credentials are server-side env (FISHHAWKD_JIRA_BASE_URL / FISHHAWKD_JIRA_EMAIL / FISHHAWKD_JIRA_API_TOKEN), NOT in this checked-in config — matching the FISHHAWKD_PROJECTS_TOKEN single-instance, secrets-never-in-repo precedent.

project_key.description: Jira project key (e.g. FISH) that filed issues are created under.

issue_types.description: Optional map from canonical work-item type (bug, feature, chore, adr, …) to the Jira issue-type name (Bug, Story, Task, …). An absent entry falls back to a title-cased default in the provider.

parent_field.description: Field used to link a created issue to its parent epic. Team-managed (next-gen) projects use the default `parent` reference ({"parent":{"key":"EPIC-KEY"}}); company-managed (classic) projects set the instance's epic-link custom field id (e.g. customfield_10014) to the bare epic key string. Optional; absent means the team-managed `parent` default.

complexity_levels.description: The complexity prior: concrete file/coupling definitions for each level, so a filer picks one deterministically. Optional in a repo config but shipped in the default.

required_fields.description: Fields every filed item must carry. The mandatory trio is Summary, Done-means, complexity (semantic check enforces their presence); everything else is optional. Done-means must be testable — see field_hints.

field_hints.description: Per-field authoring hints, keyed by field name. The Done-means hint must state that the condition is testable.

types.description: Work-item types, keyed by snake_case type name (bug, feature, chore, adr, …). Each declares how an item of that type is materialized.

states.description: Canonical board-state map for run-lifecycle-driven transitions (#1012): each canonical state from the closed set (backlog/in_progress/in_review/blocked/done) maps to the provider's column/option string (e.g. the GitHub Projects single-select Status option "In Progress"). The small closed set is what keeps a future Jira provider tractable. Optional and additive: absent means no transitions are configured and the board-sync hook no-ops. Every transitions value must be a key present here (semantic check).

backlog.description: Provider option for the canonical backlog state.

in_progress.description: Provider option for the canonical in_progress state.

in_review.description: Provider option for the canonical in_review state.

blocked.description: Provider option for the canonical blocked state.

done.description: Provider option for the canonical done state.

transitions.description: Run-lifecycle-event to canonical-state map (#1012): when a run reaches a lifecycle point, the board card moves to the mapped canonical state (resolved to a provider option via `states`). The closed event set is run_started/pr_opened/run_failed/run_merged. Optional and additive: absent means no transitions are configured. Every value must be a key present in `states` (semantic check).

run_started.description: Canonical state when a run starts on the item (e.g. in_progress).

pr_opened.description: Canonical state when the run opens a PR (e.g. in_review).

run_failed.description: Canonical state when the run fails (e.g. blocked).

run_merged.description: Canonical state when the run's PR merges (e.g. done).

product_feedback.description: Per-repo kill-switch for upstream product-feedback egress (ADR-029, #1006). Absent means egress is enabled (the default), so an existing config keeps working without edits. With enabled:false, POST /v0/runs/{id}/product-reports returns 403 product_feedback_disabled and files nothing. The object shape mirrors the backend workmgmt.ProductFeedback struct (a boolean enabled), decoded under DisallowUnknownFields.

enabled.description: Whether upstream product-feedback egress is allowed for this repo. enabled:false hard-disables the product-report endpoint.

title_format.description: Title template with {placeholder} tokens (e.g. {summary}, {epic}, {n}, {number}). Rendering is the apply layer's concern; this schema only carries the template.

body_skeleton.description: Ordered list of body section headings. Dual-audience by design, e.g. Feature = Summary/Proposal/Where to look/Done-means/Acceptance criteria/Notes/Relations; Bug = Summary/Observed/Proposal/Where to look/Done-means/Acceptance criteria/Notes/Relations; ADR = Context/Options/Recommendation/Decision/Consequences; Chore = Summary/Done-means.

optional_sections.description: Section headings from body_skeleton that render only when the filing supplies content for them: an absent Sections key omits the heading entirely, while a present key — even one with an empty value — renders the heading in position exactly as a mandatory section does. Every entry MUST also appear in this type's body_skeleton (semantic check enforces, fail-closed). Sections not listed here render unconditionally. Additive-optional within v0.

default_labels.description: Labels applied to every item of this type before caller-supplied labels are merged.

default_fields.description: Default board placement and classification fields.

status.description: Single-select Status field value (e.g. Backlog). For GitHub Projects this is the status field set via updateProjectV2ItemFieldValue.

board_column.description: Board column, when distinct from status.

complexity.description: Default complexity prior for this type.

numbering.description: Numbering rule. Required when the type is `adr` (semantic check). ADRs number sequentially.

scheme.description: Allocation scheme. v0 supports sequential (next integer after the highest existing).

prefix.description: Number prefix rendered into the title (e.g. ADR-).

pad.description: Zero-pad the rendered {number} to this minimum width (e.g. 3 -> 041). 0/absent means no padding. Bounded 0..12 so a runaway pad cannot allocate an oversized title string; 12 exceeds any realistic numbering width.

epic_link.description: Whether items of this type link to a parent epic. required = a parent epic relation must be supplied; none = epic linking does not apply.

canonical_state.description: A canonical board state from the closed set. The provider option string each maps to is declared in `states`.

label.description: A repo label, either a bare token (epic, adr) or a namespaced token (area:backend, type:feature, autonomy:low, phase:p0).
